data.title: Lululemon's results and rally draws mixed reviews from Wall Street analysts
data.description:
Investors breathed a sigh and poured into shares of Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) as the company’s Q3 results showed enough improvement in the company’s performance to launch shares as much as 19% higher and into the green for the 11th time in 12 days.

The company, synonymous with yoga pants and athleisure, delivered a solid quarterly earnings report with a 9% increase in sales that bolstered profits by 46% and its gross profit margin 150 basis points. While there were a few soft spots – U.S. comparable sales were down 2% -- the company enjoyed significant gains overseas.

The outcome of Q3 translated into an upbeat outlook for the remainder of the year, as well, with revenue growth pegged at 8% to 10% for Q4.

The consensus from the sell side, however, was mixed as Citi analyst Paul Lejuez warned that after years of benefitting from outsized growth in active apparel, “trends in the category have slowed with data in yoga and active apparel pointing to further deceleration in Q2 2025,” leaving Lululemon (LULU) more susceptible to promotional pricing pressures from competitors. Lejuez is also concerned with the long-term outlook for China, as “incremental weakening of the China consumer environment” is an added risk for Lululemon (LULU).

Wells Fargo’s Ike Boruchow is more cautiously optimistic on Lululemon’s (LULU) prospects and advised bulls to remain patient for an early 2025 inflection. The “no worse-than-feared” print shows that management may have their arms around merchandising and execution issues that plagued the company this year. Boruchow views the Q3 results as “more in line than better,” and views the slowdown in North America along with “over earning” profit & loss a reason to remain cautious over the next 12 months. Boruchow maintained his Equalweight rating on the stock.

No surprise, the results failed to move the needle for perennial Lululemon (LULU) bear, Randal Konik at Jefferies. “With increasing competition and fashion trends shifting away from [Lululemon’s] core offering, we advise investors to be sellers on the uptick in shares today,” he says, citing declining U.S. comparable sales, the choppy macro environment, and the men’s segment slowing to below 10% growth, a development Konik calls a “yellow flag.” Konik held onto his Underperform rating and $220 price target, which is 45% below LULU’s current price.

But for Morgan Stanley’s Alex Straton, the 2% decline in North American sales is reason to double down on her bullish thesis for Lululemon (LULU) and to up her price target by 20% to $414.

The comparable sales in the Americas suggest the region may be bottoming as strategic newness and product decisions appear to be taking hold “leaving us more confident that a re-acceleration in the business is possible in 2025,” and the conservative guidance “sets LULU up for another beat and raise in Q4,” Straton says.

“Taken together, the quarter emboldened our Overweight thesis, and we think likely shifts key debates in favor of the bulls.”

The consensus among Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors appear to agree with Straton, giving Lululemon (LULU) an average Buy rating. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, however, views Lululemon (LULU) as a Hold.

data.title: Bitcoin set to rise on weekly basis, soars 132% in a year
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is set to rise 4.32% on a weekly basis, as it finally breached the $100K threshold in the period, after hovering around it for some weeks.

The world’s largest digital currency has seen a meteoric rise since Donald Trump won the elections, and investors had been waiting for the cryptocurrency to jet past the $100K mark.

BTC started the week over $95K, and traded in a narrow range till Wednesday.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) touched the long awaited milestone as Trump’s pick of crypto backer, Paul Atkins as the SEC chair on Wednesday played the catalyst in pushing it past the mark.

The cryptocurrency, however, fell below the mark on Thursday.

“If Donald Trump's plan to create a Bitcoin strategic reserve is implemented and other countries quickly follow suit, it would significantly increase overall demand for Bitcoin. This could make Bitcoin worth $400,000 per coin within the next 5 years,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Kennan Mell.

Click here to know what other Seeking Alpha analysts are saying as Bitcoin traded above $100K.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is up a whopping 132% in a year.

"As long as Bitcoin maintains this close—over $100k—going into the end of this week, we would expect that it continues to run higher, and could easily see it between $110k and $120k this month and going into next year," Cory Klippsten, CEO of Swan, a Bitcoin financial services firm, said.

With the cryptocurrency’s rise, it is becoming one of the most prominent "Trump trades" in the aftermath of the 2024 election. Since the polls, Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) price has jumped ~50%, as it benefits from rise in demand, increase in ETF inflows and a positive investor sentiment fuelled by Trump’s win.

Wednesday also saw robust inflows into the spot BTC ETFs with $556.8 million coming in as net inflows, according to data from Farside Investors.

"As we look ahead, Bitcoin's potential for growth remains significant, especially as institutions recognize it as a reliable store of value and increasingly invest in Bitcoin ETFs. Additionally, we anticipate interest in Ethereum ETFs and the forthcoming approval of Solana and Ripple ETFs, which will further diversify institutional portfolios," said Jeff Mei, COO at BTSE.

Companies such as MicroStrategy (MSTR) have also been on a Bitcoin purchasing spree, and more companies have been looking to join the bandwagon, leading to a rise in corporate adoption of the cryptocurrency.

Institutional flows into Bitcoin (BTC-USD) are expected to continue in 2025, which would make the cryptocurrency reach $200,000 by the end of next year, said Geoff Kendrick, head of Crypto Research at Standard Chartered Bank, in a note.

"I think the prospects for next year also look excellent, though this depends a lot more on whether we get the strategic Bitcoin reserve and other political outcomes, with some these outcomes potentially sending Bitcoin into the hundreds of thousands of dollars per coin,” Klippsten added.

Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) market cap now stands at $1.97 trillion.

"Technical analysis would point to about 120K as the next immediate resistance, and we would expect to see more major altcoins start to outperform in the near term as the FOMO appetite spreads out," said Augustine Fan, Head of Insights, SOFA.

Notable News

CleanSpark (CLSK) mined 622 bitcoins in November, while Argo Blockchain (ARBK) Wednesday said that it mined 39 Bitcoin in the month. Riot Platforms (RIOT) produced 495 Bitcoin in November. MARA mined 26.7% more Bitcoin in the month.

MARA (MARA) said it will acquire a wind farm in Hansford County, Texas. The firm announced the pricing of its offering of $850 million aggregate principal amount of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2031.

Nano Labs (NA) said it plans to purchase and hold $50 million worth of bitcoin (BTC-USD) over the next five years.

The U.S. will likely approve cryptocurrency legislation "fairly quickly" after crypto-friendly Donald Trump becomes president, Coinbase's (COIN) policy chief Faryar Shirzad told CNBC.

Coinbase (COIN) added Apple Pay (AAPL) for all fiat-to-crypto purchases to its Onramp product for onboarding to crypto, as it seeks to streamline what can be a cumbersome process.

Bitcoin, Ether prices

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) up 2.58% to ~$101.70K at 3.05 pm on Friday. Ether (ETH-USD) rose 6.40% to $4.07K.

data.title: Bloom Energy price target hiked to Street high at Susquehanna, seeing data center momentum
data.description:
Bloom Energy (NYSE:BE) +4.1% in Friday's trading as Susquehanna reiterates its Positive rating while raising its price target to a Street-high $33 from $20, to reflect the company's momentum in supplying fuel cells to power data centers and other large industrial customers.

"Onsite generation using Bloom's fuel cell technology is emerging as a real alternative to traditional grid power for consumers who are struggling to secure grid power in a timely manner," Susquehanna's Biju Perincheril writes.

The move comes three weeks after American Electric Power (AEP) said it would use up to 1 GW of Bloom's (BE) fuel cells to power data centers, which the analyst says should pave the way for additional large utility-level agreements.

The order "serves as a proof point for Bloom's fuel cells as a viable option for data centers looking for quick access to power in the face of significant grid constraints on power availability," Perincheril writes, believing "this method of utilities using Bloom fuel cells for distributed generation will be increasingly common."

data.title: Fed's Mary Daly says jobs report shows "labor market remains in a good position'
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly on Friday said the November jobs report released earlier in the day shows the “labor market remains in a good position.”

The Bureau of Labor Statistics earlier in the day reported that nonfarm payrolls last month rose by 227K, ahead of the Street’s view for 211K. The unemployment rate, as expected, ticked up to 4.2% from 4.1% in October.

Daly said during a talk at Stanford’s University’s Hoover Institution that data showed there was a “balanced labor market” in that there was around one vacancy per available worker.

“And against that backdrop, we’re also seeing just a general trend in the United States that firms still want more workers than they can find,” she said. “And they’re turning to technology to augment their workforce, [and to] make their workforce better and more productive.”

Meanwhile, Daly said the Fed’s approach to its dual mandate will remain despite the upcoming change of administrations. “Administrations change, but our goals are always the same: price stability, full unemployment,” she said.

Daly declined to “speculate” on President-elect Donald Trump’s stated intentions to use tariffs against other countries because he has not taken office yet.

Also, Daly declined to comment on what the Federal Open Market Committee would decide at its next rate-setting meeting on Dec. 17-18, saying the meeting was too close.

The FOMC’s so-called blackout period when participants do not comment on policy begins on Saturday, and Daly is a voting member of the FOMC this year.

More on the US Economy

  • November nonfarm payrolls rebound from October's storms, strike; unemployment touches 4.2%
  • Will A Solid U.S. Jobs Report Dampen Expectation For A Fed Cut This Month?
  • Initial jobless claims increase more than expected in past week
  • Is U.S. Economic Growth Accelerating In Q4?
  • U.S. economic activity rises slightly since late October: Fed's Beige Book

data.title: Haemonetics started at overweight by J.P. Morgan
data.description:
J.P. Morgan has initiated coverage of Haemonetics (NYSE:HAE) with an overweight rating, citing the potential for topline acceleration.

The investment bank said it viewed Haemonetics (HAE) as having “multiple shots on goal that should support a top-line acceleration and healthy cash flow generation over the coming years as the mix shifts to premium, higher margin interventional products and the commercial Plasma business normalizes.”

J.P. Morgan noted that the stock was trading at an approximately 3-4x turn discount to peers, which it thinks offers a “compelling entry point with visibility into a long-term compounder driven by a three-pronged formula of premium MedTech top-line/earnings growth, potential multiple expansion, and opportunistic buy-backs.”

The investment bank set its price target for the stock at $116.

data.title: U.S. rig count shoots higher after declining for three straight weeks
data.description:
Active rigs drilling for oil and gas in the U.S. increased by 7 to 589, the highest since mid-September, after declining by a single rig for three three consecutive weeks, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest report.

Drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S.rose by 5 to 482, gas rigs gained 2 to 102, and 5 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin added 1 to 301, stayed flat at 40 in the Eagle Ford, and gained 1 to 35 in the Williston Basin.

Despite this week's increase, Baker Hughes said the total rig count was down by 37, or 6%, compared with the same time a year ago.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Sagimet started at outperform by Oppenheimer, MASH drug cited
data.description:
Shares of Sagimet Biosciences (NASDAQ:SGMT) rose 7% Friday after Oppenheimer started coverage of the stock with an outperform rating, citing the company's lead asset denifanstat.

Oppenheimer said it sees an “underappreciated opportunity” for denifanstat in the treatment of MASH. The bank sees the potential market for MASH at around $15B.

“We anticipate a near-term catalyst upon SGMT (SGMT) raising ~$200M additional capital for ongoing Phase 3 registrational trials by leveraging denifanstat's competitive profile based on Phase 2b data,” the investment bank said in a note.

Jefferies also sees Sagimet (SGMT) benefitting from rival Madrigal’s (MDGL) build-out of the MASH market. It added that Sagimet’s (SGMT) partnership with Ascletis (OTCPK:ASCLF) “could provide additional value creation with Phase 3 readouts for denifanstat in glioblastoma expected in 1H25 and acne in 2H25.”

The bank set its price target for the stock at $30.

data.title: Meta unveils new Llama 3.3 AI model
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) took the wraps off its latest large language model, known as Llama 3.3, on Friday.

Meta shares rose 2.5% in midday trading on Friday.

Llama 3.3 is a 70B parameter model that is able to deliver the same performance as the company's 405B model, "but is easier & more cost-efficient to run," Meta AI executive Ahmad Al-Dahle said in a post on X.

Al-Dahle also showed that the new Llama 3.3 can outperform several other AI models, including Google’s (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini 1.5 Pro, OpenAI’s GPT-4o and Amazon's (AMZN) Nova Pro, which the tech giant announced at its AWS re:Invent conference this week.

Llama 3.3 is available for download already, Meta added.

The release of the new AI model comes just a few months after Meta released its Llama 3.2 model in September. That model has mini versions available, which allows them to fit on edge and mobile devices.

Meta is also hard at work on its upcoming Llama models, known as Llama 4. Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg talked up the Llama 4 models on the company's third-quarter earnings call and said they are "being trained on a cluster that is bigger than 100,000 H100s or bigger than anything that I've seen reported for what others are doing."

data.title: U.S. Senators urge investigation into anti-competitive behavior by DraftKings, FanDuel
data.description:
U.S. Senators Mike Lee and Peter Welch are urging the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and Department of Justice to open an investigation into DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG) and FanDuel (NYSE:FLUT) over concerns that the two online betting platforms are engaged in anticompetitive behavior.

“After their merger to monopoly was blocked, it seems that FanDuel and DraftKings have arguably acted as one company, violating our antitrust laws…and have undertaken a coordinated effort to pressure crucial business partners not to do business with these new players,” the Senators’ letter says.

Lee and Welch accuse FanDuel (FLUT) and DraftKings (DKNG) of interfering with rivals’ relationships with sports leagues, marketing partners, payment processing companies, and “critical” vendors, and ask the FTC and DOJ to give “serious attention” to these allegations.

In 2016, the two sports betting platforms’ attempt to merge was blocked by the FTC, District of Columbia, and California as the combined company would control 90% of the online betting market. Lee and Welch claim the two companies subsequently leveraged their dominance in fantasy sports to become “online sports betting giants,” and are undermining new competitors’ efforts to challenge their dominance.

Flutter Entertainment (FLUT), the parent company of FanDuel, Betfair, Sportsbet, PokerStars and Sky Betting and Gaming, is the largest online sports betting platform with a 47% share of the U.S. online sportsbook market.

As the third-largest sports betting platforms, DraftKings (DKNG) has 2.1M average monthly users and operates in 21 states.

Shares of DraftKings (DKNG) fell as much as 7% on the news but have since reversed some of the losses to trade unchanged from Thursday’s close. Flutter (FLUT) fell 4%, snapping back to unchanged on the day.
